steps.title: Combine the radicals
steps.content:
Since $a$ and $c$ are positive, $a+c$ is positive. Combine the radicals:
$$\sqrt{(a+c)^3}\cdot\sqrt{a+c}=\sqrt{(a+c)^3(a+c)}=\sqrt{(a+c)^4}$$

steps.title: Simplify the power
steps.content:
Taking the square root of the fourth power gives
$$\sqrt{(a+c)^4}=(a+c)^2$$
